hooks.prompt:
A git commit is about to run: $ARGUMENTS

First look at the tool call's command above: if it does not actually execute `git commit`, reply allow immediately without running anything.

Review the STAGED changes before allowing it. Run `git diff --cached --stat`, then `git diff --cached`. Read surrounding code only if needed to judge.

Check against this repo's rules (CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/):
1. Layer boundaries — lib/src/domain stays pure Dart (no Firebase imports); data owns Firebase details; application orchestrates; features is UI only. No direct Firestore .collection() calls in features/, no booking status transitions performed client-side.
2. Hygiene — no hardcoded secrets or API keys, no leftover debug prints, no large commented-out code blocks.
3. Regression bar — a bug fix must include (or demonstrably already have) a regression test; new or changed business logic in lib/src/{domain,data,application} should come with tests in the same commit.

DENY only on clear violations: a committed secret, a broken layer boundary, client-side booking status mutation, a bug fix with no regression test, or new critical-layer logic with zero tests. Otherwise ALLOW — for style nits or when uncertain, allow and briefly mention the concern.
